title: geranium cinereum subsp. subargenteum
intro: Geranium cinereum subsp. subargenteum, also known as the Mountain Cranesbill or Grey-leaved Geranium, is a flowering plant species within the Geranium genus, characterized by its greyish-green leaves and pink-purple flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and menstrual issues. The health benefits of Geranium cinereum subsp. subargenteum include its ability to reduce stress, anxiety, and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and improve sleep quality. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
